content.body: This document exists to help agents choose how much UX detail to create for a given work type. Use it before authoring detailed UX items so the package declares its profile, target depth, required facets, optional facets, and allowed omissions.

content.title: Package Manifest First
body: Every meaningful UX package should start with a manifest. The manifest records:
- spec profile
- depth target
- UX modes
- included facets
- excluded facets
- allowed omissions
- completion standard
- governing artifacts
The manifest prevents agents from treating every UX task as a full minute-detail spec.

content.title: Depth Levels
body: `L0: Feature Overview` covers feature purpose, user value, major surfaces, entry/exit points, outcomes, constraints, and non-goals.
`L1: Journey And Flow` covers journeys, flows, subflows, happy paths, alternate paths, blocked paths, and recovery paths.
`L2: Requirement` covers governing App Behavior requirements for meaningful behavior concepts.
`L3: Observable Scenario And Edge Case` covers loading, empty, error, invalid, dirty, stale, auth, recovery, edge cases, and must-not behavior.
`L4: Acceptance Criteria And Proof` covers testable proof expectations tied to requirements, scenarios, or subflows.

`L5: Minute Detail` covers exact copy when governing, validation copy and placement, focus/keyboard behavior, responsive behavior, accessibility, permissions, persistence, navigation, all known edge cases, and detailed acceptance criteria.

content.title: Facet Vocabulary
body: Use these facets in manifests. See [Artifact Roles And Facets](08-artifact-roles-and-facets.md) for the canonical definition of each facet and [Granularity And Coverage](09-granularity-and-coverage.md) for coverage-index rules.
- `features`
- `subfeatures`
- `journeys`
- `flows`
- `subflows`
- `requirements`
- `observableScenarios`
- `validation`
- `recovery`
- `emptyLoadingError`
- `copy`
- `layoutSemantics`
- `accessibility`
- `permissions`
- `persistence`
- `navigation`
- `edgeCases`
- `acceptanceCriteria`
- `currentDesiredMappings`
- `pendingDecisions`

content.title: Full Product UX Spec
body: Use `full-product-ux-spec` for broad, important product areas where agents need a complete behavior model.
Required modes: `desired`; add `current`, `ideal`, or `pending-confirmation` when relevant.
Required depth: at least `L4`.
Required facets: features, subfeatures, journeys, flows, subflows, requirements, observableScenarios, acceptanceCriteria, pendingDecisions.
Optional facets: copy, layoutSemantics, accessibility, permissions, persistence, navigation, currentDesiredMappings.
Allowed omissions: minute copy, pixel-level layout, implementation proof details, and unrelated adjacent features.
Completion standard: another agent can understand the product area and implement from desired UX without inventing major behavior.

content.title: Refactor Preservation Spec
body: Use `refactor-preservation-spec` before meaningful refactor work on existing UX.
Required modes: `current`, `confirmed-current`, `desired`; add `intermediate` when migration or staged rollout affects UX.
Required depth: at least `L4`.
Required facets: journeys, flows, subflows, requirements, observableScenarios, acceptanceCriteria, currentDesiredMappings, pendingDecisions.
Optional facets: ideal, copy, layoutSemantics, accessibility, persistence, navigation.
Allowed omissions: ideal redesign, unrelated UX improvements, minute copy unless behavior-changing, and pixel-level visual detail.
Completion standard: preserved behavior, changed behavior, pending behavior, and proof gates are explicit before implementation.

content.title: New Feature Spec
body: Use `new-feature-spec` when no current UX exists.
Required modes: `desired`; add `ideal` when exploring best possible UX before selecting target behavior.
Required depth: at least `L3`, or `L4` for important shipped features.
Required facets: features or subfeatures, journeys, flows, requirements, observableScenarios, acceptanceCriteria.
Optional facets: pendingDecisions, copy, layoutSemantics, accessibility, permissions, persistence, navigation.
Allowed omissions: current UX, currentDesiredMappings, migration-only details.
Completion standard: desired UX is complete enough for implementation and proof without relying on future product invention.

content.title: Feature Extension Spec
body: Use `feature-extension-spec` when adding behavior to an existing feature.
Required modes: `current`, `desired`; add `pending-confirmation` for behavior changes and `confirmed-current` for preserved touched behavior.
Required depth: at least `L3`.
Required facets: affected journeys, affected flows, requirements, observableScenarios, currentDesiredMappings, acceptanceCriteria, pendingDecisions.
Optional facets: broader feature overview, ideal, copy, layoutSemantics, accessibility.
Allowed omissions: unaffected feature areas and unrelated existing flows.
Completion standard: the extension is clearly integrated with existing UX without redefining unaffected behavior.

content.title: UX Audit Spec
body: Use `ux-audit-spec` to document what exists today.
Required modes: `current` and `unknown`; add `suspected accidental` through status/authority where appropriate.
Required depth: at least `L2`, with `L3` for complex flows.
Required facets: journeys, flows, requirements, observableScenarios, pendingDecisions for unknowns.
Optional facets: desired, currentDesiredMappings, acceptanceCriteria.
Allowed omissions: desired UX decisions and implementation plans.
Completion standard: current UX is discoverable with confidence levels and unresolved gaps are routed.

For exhaustive full-app current UX investigation, use this as a route/source-first audit profile: declare current, observed-current, uncertain-current, and unknown modes in the package manifest; include route inventory, surface decomposition, hidden UX inventory, runtime observations, current requirements, observable scenarios, coverage rows, and pending decisions; and follow the JSON-only investigation guidance in [Investigation System](../50-investigation/00-investigation-system.doc.json). Desired UX remains out of scope except for pending recommendations or explicit follow-up decisions.

content.title: Test Contract Spec
body: Use `test-contract-spec` when proof is the main deliverable.
Required modes: `desired`; add `confirmed-current` for refactor preservation.
Required depth: `L4`.
Required facets: requirements, observableScenarios, acceptanceCriteria.
Optional facets: journeys and flows for context, currentDesiredMappings when preserving existing behavior.
Allowed omissions: feature overview if already present, broad product rationale, and visual details not needed for proof.
Completion standard: tests can be written or reviewed against explicit UX acceptance criteria.

content.title: Minute Detail Spec
body: Use `minute-detail-spec` when exactness matters because risk, regulation, high traffic, conversion, accessibility, or user trust requires it.
Required modes: `desired`; add current or intermediate as needed.
Required depth: `L5`.

Required facets: all relevant facets, especially validation, recovery, emptyLoadingError, copy, layoutSemantics, accessibility, permissions, persistence, navigation, edgeCases, and acceptanceCriteria.
Allowed omissions: only explicitly out-of-scope adjacent behavior.
Completion standard: a future agent can implement and test the behavior to minute observable detail without asking for UX clarification.

content.title: Lightweight Spec
body: Use `lightweight-spec` for small, low-risk behavior where full hierarchy would add noise.
Required modes: `desired`; add `current` only if changing existing behavior.
Required depth: `L2`, or `L3` if edge cases are meaningful.
Required facets: affected requirements and acceptanceCriteria.
Optional facets: journey, flow, observableScenarios.
Allowed omissions: feature overview, full journey map, unaffected flows, minute detail.
Completion standard: the small change is clear enough to implement and verify without creating duplicate UX authority.
